mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate earnings downgrades, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2 percentage points in 30 calendar days
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in consumer credit outstanding and industrial production indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when tested against a random baseline, or the interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust real GDP growth, rising housing starts amplify consumer wealth effects and construction sector profitability, producing sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when housing starts rise alongside GDP growth above 3% annualized, indicating the wealth effect mechanism breaks down in overheating conditions.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index reflects improving supply chain throughput and capacity utilization, which lowers input cost inflation and supports corporate margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or INDPRO increases coincide with SPX declines in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a 4.5 percent threshold, the cost of capital suppresses equity valuations and reduces leveraged corporate cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity valuations remain stable or expand when the policy rate breaches 4.5 percent, or forward returns show no statistically significant decline relative to pre-threshold periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real GDP expansion signals a self-reinforcing economic cycle that boosts aggregate demand and corporate revenue, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts positive returns but SPX delivers negative returns in over 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient becomes insignificant after controlling for inflation.

mechanism_narrative: In a stagflationary environment where consumer credit growth stalls while inflation remains elevated, equity risk premiums expand rapidly, causing sharp negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive despite stagnant credit growth and high inflation, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in walk-forward testing over a 10-year period.
